steps.title: Clear the fraction
steps.content:
Start with the given equation:
$$ak = \frac{b}{12}(8+m)$$
Since $b$ is positive, multiply both sides by $\frac{12}{b}$ to isolate the parenthetical expression:
$$\frac{12ak}{b} = 8+m$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $m=\frac{12(ak-8)}{b}$ — incorrect; this would require subtracting $8$ before dividing by $b$, but the $8$ should be subtracted AFTER multiplying by $\frac{12}{b}$
  • B: $m=ak-8-\frac{b}{12}$ — incorrect; the $\frac{b}{12}$ should be inverted to $\frac{12}{b}$ and multiplied by $ak$, not subtracted
  • C: $m=ak-\frac{8b}{12}$ — incorrect; same error as B, the fraction was not properly cleared by multiplying by its reciprocal
  • D: $m=\frac{12ak}{b}-8$ — matches our derivation exactly
That matches choice D — D is correct.
